install.summary: Open-source AI agent by Nous Research (MIT). ONE install gives the Electron desktop GUI AND the `hermes` CLI on PATH — same repo, not two products.
install.default_path: Run the OFFICIAL installer (Hermes-Setup.exe on Windows / .dmg on macOS / the terminal install on Linux) from https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/desktop. This is the PRIMARY path and works for the majority of users (overseas / unrestricted networks — the core audience).
install.official_is_a_bootstrapper: The official installer is a thin bootstrapper that fetches scripts/install.ps1 (or install.sh) from raw.githubusercontent.com, then builds: uv -> Python 3.11 -> Git -> Node.js 22 -> clone repo -> Python/Node deps -> Electron desktop build -> Playwright Chromium -> PATH. In mainland China WITHOUT a VPN it can hang (raw.githubusercontent / the dep CDNs are GFW-blocked, e.g. stuck at '0 of 0 steps'). That case is handled by the user-triggered FALLBACK in install_flow — NOT by probing the network up front (a reachable github does not predict success, and probing is unreliable on the intermittent GFW).

cli.note: The same install also yields a `hermes` CLI on PATH (oneshot: `hermes chat -q "<message>" --quiet`). Primary user-facing form is the desktop GUI; the CLI is a bonus, not separately maintained.

install_flow.summary: DEFAULT: run the official installer (works for the overseas / unrestricted majority — the core users). When you launch it, PROACTIVELY tell the user that if it fails or hangs (common in mainland China without a VPN), they should say so — and you will then re-install via the China-mirror method (the FALLBACK steps). Do NOT probe GitHub reachability to choose a path: just default to the official installer and let a user-reported failure trigger the mirror fallback.
agent_steps: Desktop app — LOCAL machine only. If connected to a remote server, switch to the local server first.

DEFAULT — run the OFFICIAL installer (do NOT probe the network first): download Hermes-Setup.exe (Windows) / the .dmg (macOS) from https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/desktop or the latest GitHub release asset into the Downloads folder, then LAUNCH it DETACHED so the wizard appears (Windows: `Start-Process "$env:USERPROFILE\Downloads\Hermes-Setup.exe"`; macOS: `open <dmg>`) and hand off. On Linux, run the official terminal install.

PROACTIVE FALLBACK OFFER — at the same time you launch it, tell the user plainly (in their language): the official installer is running, click through it; BUT if it fails or hangs — common in mainland China without a VPN, it can stick at '0 of 0 steps' — just tell me and I'll re-install it via the China-mirror method instead. Then STOP and hand off; do NOT keep polling or running `--version` to check.
FALLBACK — run the steps A-D below ONLY when the user reports the official installer failed / hung / could not download. Same robust mirror flow, just on demand instead of by default.

STEP A — set China package mirrors as environment variables for the install session: PyPI -> `UV_DEFAULT_INDEX=https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple` and `PIP_INDEX_URL=https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple`; uv's Python download -> `UV_PYTHON_INSTALL_MIRROR=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/python-build-standalone`; npm -> `npm_config_registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com`; Node.js portable -> `https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/node/`; Electron -> `ELECTRON_MIRROR=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/electron/`; Playwright -> `PLAYWRIGHT_DOWNLOAD_HOST=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/playwright`.

STEP B — route git through a reachable GitHub proxy so the repo clone (and any github.com release downloads) succeed: `git config --global url."https://<working-gh-proxy>/https://github.com/".insteadOf "https://github.com/"`. GitHub proxies (ghproxy-class) are volatile — pick one that currently responds; or pre-install Git (`winget install Git.Git`), or use npmmirror's `git-for-windows` mirror.

STEP C — fetch the install SCRIPT from a China-reachable GitHub mirror instead of the blocked raw.githubusercontent.com: Windows `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/NousResearch/hermes-agent@main/scripts/install.ps1`, mac/Linux the same path ending `/install.sh`. If jsdelivr is unreachable on this network, use a ghproxy-class raw proxy.

STEP D — run the fetched script with the STEP A env vars set and the STEP B git proxy configured. Its github.com calls are redirected by the insteadOf rule; its pip / npm / node / electron / playwright downloads by the env vars. Watch the install actually PROGRESS — if it stalls on a hop, read the log to find the blocked host and point that one host at its mirror.

BOUNDED LATITUDE + SECURITY: mirrors (jsdelivr / gh-proxy) come and go — if a hop fails, find an alternative working mirror at runtime rather than giving up; you may improvise. BUT the script you run is the OFFICIAL Hermes repo content fetched via a mirror — make sure the mirror serves the genuine repo (do not run a modified/untrusted script), and NEVER recommend a specific commercial VPN.
VERIFY: after install, `hermes --version` confirms the CLI is on PATH; the Electron desktop app is built under %LOCALAPPDATA%\hermes (Windows) / the app bundle (macOS).
Tell the user plainly what was installed (Hermes desktop GUI + `hermes` CLI), where, and how to launch it.
tell_user: Which path was used — the official installer (default) or the China-mirror fallback
Upfront, when launching the official installer: that they should report a failure/hang so you can switch to the mirror method
That ONE install gives both the desktop GUI and the `hermes` CLI
Install location and how to launch

network_requirements.note: These hosts are what the build TOUCHES — NOT a checklist to probe-and-gate on. Do NOT decide the path by testing them; default to the official installer and use the mirror FALLBACK only when the user reports a failure. Listed so you know which host maps to which mirror if a fallback hop stalls.

blocked_regions.zh-CN: The official Hermes-Setup.exe is a bootstrapper that fetches scripts/install.ps1 from raw.githubusercontent.com — ~always GFW-blocked in mainland China (os error 10054 / RST). The build also pulls from github.com (clone, PortableGit), PyPI, nodejs.org, npm, the Electron CDN and the Playwright CDN — most throttled/blocked in CN. Fallback bypass: fetch the script via jsdelivr/ghproxy and set China package mirrors (Tsinghua PyPI; npmmirror for node/npm/electron/playwright/python-build-standalone; a GitHub proxy for the git clone). See install_flow.agent_steps STEP A-D.

mirrors.github_raw: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/NousResearch/hermes-agent@main/ (jsdelivr; or a ghproxy-class raw proxy, volatile — verify at runtime)
mirrors.github_clone: git insteadOf via a ghproxy-class proxy (volatile — verify at runtime)
